channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KREX 5

item.title: Kansas once required voters to prove citizenship. That didn't work out so well
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republicans made claims about illegal voting by noncitizens a centerpiece of their 2024 campaign messaging and plan to push legislation in the new Congress requiring voters to provide proof of U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KTLA 5

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 15 hurt in Florida when train hits fire truck that drove onto tracks after another train passed
item.description: Three firefighters and a dozen passengers were injured in Florida on Saturday when a fire truck with its lights flashing drove around rail crossing arms and into the path of a high-speed passenger train after waiting for another train to pass, according to video of the incident and a person briefed
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: These are America's safest small towns: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More battles over transgender rights await Supreme Court
item.description: Cases implicating transgender rights are piling up at the Supreme Court as it begins drafting its opinion on whether states can ban gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: The Tesla CEO said he has the “right” to address the country’s political climate as he has “made significant investments” in Germany’s technological and industrial sectors.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Abortions are up in the US. It's a complicated picture as women turn to pills, travel
item.description: Abortion has become slightly more common despite bans or deep restrictions in most Republican-controlled states, and the legal and political fights over its future are not over yet. It's now been two and a half years since the U.S. Supreme Court overturned Roe v.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and KEVIN S. VINEYS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oldest living survivor of attack on Pearl Harbor dies at 105
item.description: Warren Upton, the oldest living survivor of the 1941 Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor and the last remaining survivor of the USS Utah, has died at age 105.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of the 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73. Hussey died on Friday, “peacefully at home surrounded by her loved ones,” a statement posted to her Instagram account said.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serial killer Richard Ramirez’s bride a ‘superfan’ who put herself ‘top of the line’ for his love: pal
item.description: Richard Ramirez, the serial killer who was sentenced to death following his heinous crimes in California, died while on death row in 2013. He was 53.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year's Times Square New Year's Eve ball were installed on Friday morning, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge scolds Jay-Z's lawyer over 'attacks' on rape accuser's attorney
item.description: Judge Analisa Torres chastised the lawyer representing Jay-Z for what she described as his combative motions and “inflammatory language” against the plaintiff's lawyer,
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, WGN 9

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than a third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in debt this holiday season: report
item.description: (KRON) -- More than one third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in credit card debt this holiday season, according to a new report. The report from LendingTree indicates that 36% of Americans took on an average of $1,181 in debt, up from $1,028 in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Fox News hosts Sean Hannity, Ainsley Earhardt announce engagement
item.description: "We are overjoyed and so thankful to our families for all of their love and support during this wonderful time in our lives," the couple said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First severe human bird flu case shows mutations: CDC
item.description: Health experts are now warning pet owners to be cautious about raw foods and limit pets' exposure to wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: Musk stirs pot on X over 'shortage' of talented American engineers
item.description: Musk, an immigrant from South Africa, posted to X that there is a shortage of "talented" engineers in the U.S. and companies should start looking to other countries.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cat food distributed to California recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ukrainian refugee finds peace through Tarpon Springs color guard
item.description: The holidays bring a lot of happiness for many people, especially one Tarpon Springs High School student who is thankful she can celebrate in safety here in America.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé's performance highlighted Netflix's NFL debut on Christmas Day
item.description: Beyoncé provided more excitement than either game during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. Riding into her halftime appearance on a white horse, the 32-time Grammy winner rocked her hometown Houston crowd with a nearly 13-minute performance on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4
